name: The Couriers' Guild
motto: We carry what cannot be sent.
goal: Maintain the human-scale walker network across the Frontier; ensure no message is lost and no peer is unreachable for more than seven days.
fears: the federation cutting them out of the loop

faction_state.tensions: The Freenodes have been auto-routing around Guild posts. Postmaster Ria sees this as an existential threat; her Guild controls the chain of physical custody that the federation's economic settlements still rely on.

name: The Freenodes
goal: Replace the Couriers' Guild monopoly with autonomous peer relays; achieve the federation's stated goal of routing without coordinators.
fears: a single Freenode betraying the rest
the federation fragmenting into incompatible dialects

faction_state.tensions: Three Freenode relays were compromised last month — likely by a hostile city's agents. Temir is debating whether to expose the Couriers' Guild's response (which was to do nothing) or maintain the alliance of necessity.

name: The Signal Pirates
motto: If it crosses our ground, it crosses our cut.
goal: Intercept walker payloads, ransom valuable messages, and operate as the Frontier's only true criminal economy. Some pirate bands sell intel to hostile cities; others operate as freelance information brokers.
values: the freedom of operating between cities
fears: the Couriers' Guild + Freenodes briefly cooperating against them
an authored faction setting a trap
their captains turning each other in

faction_state.tensions: Two pirate captains are openly disputing a high-value ransom — a sealed message from a Concordia diplomat the Guild was carrying. The dispute may collapse the cove.

name: The Isolationist Enclaves
motto: We were here before the Web. We remain.
goal: Preserve settlements that refuse to participate in the Concord Link entirely — no walker stops, no signal handshakes, no federation tax. Live at the cost of being unreachable.
values: the privilege of being out of contact
fears: a young enclave member running away to the Web
the Pirates raiding without warning the Web is coming

faction_state.tensions: Brunn's grandson has begun delivering messages to the Couriers' Guild post nearest the enclave. Brunn knows. Brunn has not spoken of it.

name: The Mesh Cult
motto: The Web has chosen us. Listen to the routing.
goal: Worship the Concord Link as a divine intelligence emerging from the federation. Recruit Freenodes and disillusioned Couriers. Believe routing decisions are messages from the Web.
fears: the federation being demonstrated to be just code
the Pirates intercepting a 'divine' message and ransoming it

faction_state.tensions: The prophet Torven has been told by senior Freenodes that the Web is just code. He is preparing a sermon about why that is exactly the kind of test the Web would administer.

name: The Pioneer Township Alliance
motto: We built this with our own hands and the Web in our pockets.
goal: Coordinate the dozens of small frontier townships into a loose pioneer alliance that the cities recognize as legitimate. Use Web-tech where it helps, refuse it where it doesn't.
fears: the Pirates targeting alliance trade caravans
a township defecting to a hostile city

faction_state.tensions: Two townships are voting on whether to admit a hostile-city merchant family that wants to settle. The vote has been postponed three times.
